headline: East Malaysia MPs Propose Separate Time Zone for Borneo
context: A group of 18 East Malaysian MPs submitted motion in February 2026 to create separate time zone 30 minutes ahead of Peninsula.

cards.big: 18 MPs propose separate time zone for East Malaysia 30 minutes ahead
cards.sub: Motion argues sunrise at 5:45am wastes productive daylight in Borneo

cards.big: Sabah and Sarawak geographically closer to Philippines time zone GMT+8:30
cards.sub: Current unified GMT+8 means Borneo loses approximately 45 minutes evening daylight

cards.big: Economic study estimated RM280 million annual savings from reduced electricity use
cards.sub: Extended daylight benefits agriculture tourism and outdoor work sectors

cards.big: Malaysia last adjusted its time zone in 1982 to unify Peninsula and Borneo
cards.sub: Government said proposal would face administrative and business complications

cards.big: Time zone proposals test whether practical differences between regions are taken seriously
A separate Borneo time zone is symbolic, but symbols of autonomy carry real political weight.
